text: A chalice of obsidian ceramic cradles the steaming, ebony nectar, a swirling vortex of roasted darkness that exhales a tempestuous, aromatic shroud of charred bean and velvet smoke.

The cavernous, soot-stained cathedral of industry shuddered under the bruised, amethyst weight of an encroaching twilight, where the dying sun bled its vermillion lifeblood across the jagged, iron silhouettes of the skeletal rafters.
Within this liquid abyss, tendrils of alabaster cream descend like ghostly, diaphanous silk, weaving a labyrinthine tapestry of sepia and ivory amidst the bitter, obsidian depths.
Each sip is a torrential awakening, a thundering symphony of scorched earth and silken heat that ravages the slumbering senses with its scorched, caffeinated benediction.

A frantic, cacophonous sea of spectral commuters—a churning, kaleidoscopic tide of velvet capes and weeping faces—surged through the gloaming, their desperate footfalls echoing like the rhythmic, hollow thrumming of a thousand dying hearts against the obsidian floor.

Like a descent of feathered, opalescent ghosts, the pigeons descended in a frenzied, kaleidoscopic tempest of cooing cacophony, their necks shimmering with the oily, prismatic glint of bruised amethysts and spilled gasoline.

Beneath the sepulchral, amethyst shadows of a crumbling, soot-stained colonnade, the withered patriarch—a relic of antiquity draped in the moth-eaten velvet of bygone eras—extended a tremulous, liver-spotted hand toward the swirling, iridescent vortex of avian life.

Through the amber haze of gaslight and coal-smoke, the locomotive’s serpentine sighs exhaled plumes of alabaster grief, weaving a shroud of ephemeral vapor around the labyrinthine architecture of transit and woe.
Each orb of aqueous sorrow clings to the pane like a desperate, weeping specter, distorting the amber glow of the hearth-fire into a fractured, kaleidoscopic phantasmagoria of liquid grief.

As he scattered the desiccated, amber crumbs of a forgotten sustenance, the very air grew heavy with the suffocating, dusty scent of antiquity and the frantic, rhythmic beating of a thousand hollowed, translucent wings.

Through this weeping veil of weeping heavens, the domestic landscape dissolves into a smudged, impressionistic dreamscape, where the very light itself seems to drown in a rhythmic, percussive deluge of silvered despair.
The celestial weeping descends in a torrential, translucent cascade, a melancholic symphony of crystalline tears that batter against the fragile, vitreous membrane of the culinary sanctuary.

A serpentine, metal-scaled leviathan of glinting chrome and weeping lacquer lies paralyzed amidst the asphalt wasteland, its thousand-eyed headlights staring with a glazed, cataract-stricken vacancy into the smog-choked twilight.

With a heart fluttering like a caged, iridescent sparrow against the gilded bars of innocence, the tiny, wide-eyed voyager stepped across the threshold of that daunting, cavernous cathedral of erudition.

Amidst the cacophonous, symphonic roar of a thousand burgeoning intellects, the small, trembling hand sought the anchor of a maternal palm, a desperate, silken tether against the swirling, tempestuous tides of an impending, scholarly odyssey.

The morning sun, a molten, honeyed orb of celestial benevolence, spilled its liquid amber light across the polished, labyrinthine corridors, casting long, tremulous shadows of trepidation upon the pristine, unblemished visage of youth.

Each idling engine exhales a rhythmic, sulfurous sigh, weaving a shroud of grey, suffocating miasma that clings to the windshields like a funerary veil draped over the motionless, unyielding procession of human restlessness.

Within this suffocating labyrinth of stalled ambition, a cacophony of dissonant, brassy bellows erupts from the iron bellies of the machines, a discordant symphony of mechanical despair that shivers through the stagnant, exhaust-heavy ether.

Amidst the cavernous, sepulchral gloom of the hollowed corridor, where shadows stretch like bruised, velvet fingers across the desolate linoleum, stands the monolithic sentinel of gluttonous commerce, a rectangular reliquary of neon-lit decadence.

Its translucent, crystalline visage flickers with a sickly, phosphoric luminescence, casting a jaundiced, tremulous radiance upon the rows of hermetically sealed, saccharine offerings that huddle within their plastic sarcophagi.
Each rhythmic, pendulous thrum of his tail against the polished mahogany floor beat a funereal cadence, a desperate, percussive prayer offered to the silent, unyielding vacuum of the hallway.

A low, subterranean thrumming—a mechanical, rhythmic respiration of copper and cold electricity—vibrates through the stagnant, dust-moted air, singing a lonely, electronic dirge to the profound and echoing emptiness of the void.

His amber eyes, twin orbs of liquid, melancholic fire, remained perpetually tethered to the brass-studded portal, tracing the phantom geometry of shadows that danced in anticipation of a long-awaited footfall.

With a heavy, velvet-laden sigh that echoed through the cavernous, amber-lit gloom of the vestibule, the golden-maned sentinel remained anchored to the threshold, a statue of yearning carved from sinew and sorrowful patience.

Below this cosmic catastrophe, the vast, asphalt expanse—a desolate, sun-baked wasteland of petrified tar and weeping bitumen—drinks greedily of the dying light, reflecting the bruised heavens in iridescent, oil-slicked puddles of rainbowed melancholy.

Beneath the weeping, charcoal canopy of a bruised and melancholic twilight, she stood—a solitary, porcelain figurine amidst the swirling, soot-stained vapors of the urban abyss—clutching the crinkled parchment of the transit schedule as if it were a sacred, arcane codex of destiny.

The celestial orb, a hemorrhaging wound of bruised amethyst and molten, incandescent gold, descends in a slow, agonizing dissolution behind the jagged, obsidian silhouettes of skeletal telephone poles.

Each discarded aluminum can and hollowed-out sedan becomes a reliquary of shadow, drowning in a sea of lengthening, violet gloaming that smothers the mundane geometry of painted white lines under a shroud of velvet despair.

Her eyes, luminous orbs of liquid amber, traced the labyrinthine geometry of ink-black numerals and the cruel, rhythmic calligraphy of arrival times, each digit a cold, mathematical heartbeat pulsing through the paper’s fragile, fibrous veins.

The monolithic sentinels of industry—those obsidian monoliths of silicon and steel—exhale a rhythmic, sibilant sigh, a ceaseless and sonorous drone that weaves a tapestry of sonic desolation across the cavernous, shadow-drenched expanse.

Beneath the pallid, spectral luminescence of flickering fluorescent constellations, a low and melancholic thrumming vibrates through the stagnant, midnight ether, a phantom choir of mechanical respiration.

In that suspended moment of existential yearning, the mundane list of mechanical departures and arrivals transmuted into a celestial map of fleeting possibilities, a frantic litany of steel-clad chariots hurtling through the stygian gloom of the city’s indifferent, concrete arteries.
Each breath is a silken sigh, a languid undulation of velvet fur that ripples like wind-swept wheat beneath the amber, drowsy gaze of a dreaming deity.

The metal ribs, weeping with a fevered, rhythmic warmth, cradle the creature in a swaddling cocoon of thermal ecstasy, weaving a tapestry of gilded lethargy amidst the swirling, dust-mote shadows of the gloaming.

Within this hollowed cathedral of commerce, the electric pulse of unseen currents dances a macabre, microphonic waltz, an undulating murmur of static that clings to the velvet stillness like a shroud of iridescent dust.

Upon the iron altar of the slumbering radiator, the feline sovereign reclines in a state of gelatinous, honeyed repose, its sinuous form surrendered to the rhythmic, thrumming pulse of incandescent heat.

With a needle of polished bone, he weaves a frantic, rhythmic tapestry of repair, stitching together the jagged lacunae of a net that has swallowed the silvered, shivering ghosts of the fathomless abyss.

Each sinewy knot is a desperate, calcified prayer against the voracious hunger of the churning, cerulean void, an intricate embroidery of survival wrought against the relentless, thundering percussion of the encroaching tide.

Beneath the bruised and amethystine canopy of a weeping twilight, the salt-crusted patriarch sits ensconced in a cathedral of splintered driftwood, his gnarled digits dancing a melancholic ballet amidst a labyrinthine web of brine-soaked hemp.

The crystalline, alabaster shroud, once a silent and virginal mantle upon the urban arteries, now succumbs to the relentless, golden tyranny of a weeping sun, dissolving into a weeping, translucent mire.
A cyclopean monolith of porcelain catastrophe looms amidst the twilight, a calcified monument to gluttonous indulgence and the inexorable decay of domestic order.
Each snowflake, a former diamond of the firmament, surrenders its frigid sovereignty to the asphalt's obsidian maw, transmuted into a chaotic, shimmering slurry of slush and sorrowful, liquid light.

Beneath the gargantuan, soot-stained monoliths of steel and stone, the weeping gutters gurgle with the melancholic cadence of a thousand melting ghosts, as the city’s frozen breath exhales a damp and suffocating miasma of vernal decay.

Upon this jagged altar of ceramic debris, congealed remnants of a thousand forgotten feasts cling like weeping, amber-hued resins, petrifying in a grotesque mosaic of grease and starlight-dimmed grime.
The guttering, amber-veined sentinel of wax, a weeping monolith of tallow and spent ambition, descends into a melancholic, translucent abyss of its own molten making.

As the wick, that charred and ashen spine of sacrificial fiber, drowns within a viscous, opalescent sea of liquid grief, the very essence of its pale, fragrant soul dissolves into a singular, spiraling phantom of gray and spectral smoke.

Each crustaceous layer of desiccated sauce and curdled cream sings a silent, stinking dirge of negligence, weaving a tapestry of olfactory opulence that suffocates the very air in its viscous, malodorous embrace.
Its flickering, ephemeral corona—a frantic, gold-spun halo of dying luminescence—performs a desperate, rhythmic ballet against the encroaching, velvet shroud of an insatiable and obsidian night.

The parchment, a pale and ghost-stricken shroud of ivory vellum, lies entombed amidst the suffocating velvet shadows of the mahogany escritoire, its waxen seal a coagulated, crimson wound that refuses to weep its secrets.
It sits as a monument to profound, agonizing hesitation, a calcified relic of intention gathering the fine, grey silt of neglect under the indifferent, amber gaze of the dying candlelight.
Within its silent, fibrous belly, a tempest of unspoken passions and catastrophic elegies remains stifled, held captive by the cruel, unyielding stillness of an unviolated envelope.
The iron leviathan, a soot-stained behemoth of weeping steel and guttering amber, groaned its final, melancholic dirge against the frost-bitten ironies of the midnight rails.

Through the translucent, tear-streaked membranes of its carriage windows, the spectral silhouettes of weary, hollow-eyed pilgrims drifted like autumn leaves caught in a sepulchral gale, suspended in a liminal purgatory of flickering gaslight and velvet shadow.

As the locomotive exhaled a thunderous, stygian sigh of scalding steam, it vanished into the swallowing maw of the obsidian night, leaving naught but a haunting, rhythmic echo to mourn the departure of the world.
The celestial deluge has surrendered, leaving the emerald expanse to weep with the crystalline, diamond-bright residue of a thousand weeping heavens.

Each tremulous petal, heavy with the liquid opulence of the storm, bows in a melancholic, fragrant genuflection toward the loam, which exhales a petrichor so heady and primordial it seems to pulse with the very heartbeat of the quickening earth.

Through the translucent, gossamer veil of the mist, the sunlight descends in bruised, amber shafts, igniting the suspended dewdrops into a chaotic, kaleidoscopic riot of scintillating, ephemeral fire.

The clock, a gilded, cyclopean sentinel of mahogany and brass, exhales its mechanical, staccato sighs into the hollow gloom, weaving a tapestry of sonic isolation that clings to the peeling, damask-clad walls like a shroud of weeping time.

Within the sepulchral, shadow-draped sanctuary of the vacant chamber, the pendulum’s rhythmic, metronomic thrumming persists as a relentless, obsidian heartbeat, severing the velvet silence with each lacerating, brassy stroke.

Each infinitesimal click resonates through the stagnant, dust-mote-choked ether like the heavy, tolling footsteps of an invisible, inexorable phantom, marking the slow, agonizing dissolution of temporal continuity.
She immediately winced as a mindless cacophony of noise flooded through her system and she yanked them out, shooting Anith a dark look.

If it wasn't enough that I was still in deep mourning after the funeral of my father, Death himself, I had only managed to reap a pitiful *91* souls since the day of the accident.
Since fate decided for me to get the dice roll of a lifetime, I have slowly accumulated the power of the gods.
My lab coat was splattered with the nourishing tomato sauce of our one and only pasta savior.
I obsessively stared at the tupperware container in the hopes that my life-instilling contraption had fulfilled its duty.

I stared at the revolver sitting in my hand, a weapon designed to take a life, to end a life, my last chance at salvation.
Last he checked he had just passed the 20 year mark- he had sighed in a silent sadness, to think it had been this long.
Stormlights tormented the skies, teasing, needling and gouging the crooks, the crannies of deepest horizon with pitchforks of blue and white.
The thundering skies observed the unspoken lamentation of the outcast; eyeing him with the stormiest of contempt.
The last two kept an eye on the rear, poised to any attacks that may erupt out of the cloying mist.

Everything I did, every decision I made borne of logic and factual input, like a robot with a thin facade of flesh.

Her breath fills her lungs, deep and full, as if she is inhaling the totality of life with one breath.
His followers also nodded their desire to see this done, wicked daggers gleaming in the gentle lamp light of my home.
They didn't bother guarding it, to touch it was to lose all will to live and only experience the pains of life only, none of the joys.
If you’re looking for somewhere beautiful, small yet intricate, bright and bountiful, look no further than earth, your premier destination.

Sadly, John got up from his desk, abandoning his journey through the fascinating world of crabs, lobsters, crayfish, shrimp, and all the other wondering and strange things that roamed the watery worlds.
My clock said 6:29, I held it in my hand and it flipped to the next minute, sounding an abhorrent beat in my hand.

One foot plodding in front of the other as each tear and scream of the world over echoed through my soul.

In truth, an all consuming desire to lay down my burden was the one thought with any clarity amidst the haze of pain and suffering.
A cold haunting and lingering fear hanging in the icy air as I watch the familiar clouds drift upward into the abyss.
Fear, horror, dread: All of those, rolled into one big'ol ball for you to swallow down right at the end.

The sword has a muted glimmer, the glimmer reminding itself of it’s glory days of the past.
A hard crash into the ground later though and I realized, with the scrutinizing pain of my broken leg, that this was no dream.

Other people, however, are given a leftover portion of 'power'-- one that threatens to unexist itself, like an ember battered by gale-force winds.

Every time you have had an idea in your head--crystal clear and vibrant--that you just couldn't put into words, the Figure of Speech was feeding on your unexpressed imagination.
If you don't take adequate precautions, it will feast on your thoughts and rob you of your words.

Drifting in his thoughts, his past life, replaying his failures and weakness and rejoicing in successes, his wife and daughter, and all the people he helped.

Praises for your words ring through every corner of the earth, and your mission of peace has shaken the pieces of our society into place.
You came crying to me in the middle of a wintry night; your eyes tearing wildly, yet still like soft rain.
There's another reality laying just beyond the fringe of darkness, tucked away in a dreamscape inaccessible to the woken mind.
Or maybe I'm Adam, tethered to an existence certain to destroy whatever idyllic life there might be on Earth.
The senseless rape and murder that soldiers inevitably turned to as the tentacles of my empires spread caused even the process of gaining domination to turn sour.
The sun is starting to set and fear grips me, adrenaline hitting me like a crashing wave, my heart jack hammering in my chest.
